pokemon:surskit.name: Surskit
pokemon:surskit.summary: If Surskit senses danger, it secretes a thick, sugary syrup from the tip of its head. There are some Pokémon that love eating this syrup.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: If Surskit senses danger, it secretes a thick, sugary syrup from the tip of its head. There are some Pokémon that love eating this syrup.
It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.
It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.
It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.
They gather on puddles after evening downpours, gliding across the surface of water as if sliding. It secretes honey with a sweet aroma from its head.
They usually live on ponds, but after an evening shower, they may appear on puddles in towns.
It secretes a thick, sweet-scented syrup from the tip of its head. It lives on weed-choked ponds.
They usually live on ponds, but after an evening shower, they may appear on puddles in towns.
When this Pokémon senses danger, a sweet fluid oozes from the tip of its head. The taste of it disgusts bird Pokémon.
From the tips of its feet, Surskit secretes an oil that enables it to walk on water as if it were skating. This Pokémon feeds on microscopic organisms in ponds and lakes.
It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.
It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.
From the tips of its feet, SURSKIT secretes an oil that enables it to walk on water as if it were skating. This POKéMON feeds on microscopic organisms in ponds and lakes.
If SURSKIT senses danger, it secretes a thick, sugary syrup from the tip of its head. There are some POKéMON that love eating this syrup.
It secretes a thick, sweet-scented syrup from the tip of its head. It lives on weed-choked ponds.
It moves as if skating on the surface of the water. It often fights with Dewpider over food.
It lives in ponds and marshes that feature lots of plant life. It often fights with Dewpider, whose habitat and diet are similar.
If it’s in a pinch, it will secrete a sweet liquid from the tip of its head. Syrup made from gathering that liquid is tasty on bread.
It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.
It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.
They usually live on ponds, but after an evening shower, they may appear on puddles in towns.
It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: If Surskit senses danger, it secretes a thick, sugary syrup from the tip of its head. There are some Pokémon that love eating this syrup.

pokemon:masquerain.name: Masquerain
pokemon:masquerain.summary: Masquerain’s antennas have eyelike patterns that usually give it an angry look. If the “eyes” are droopy and appear sad, it is said to be a sign that a heavy rainfall is on its way.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Masquerain’s antennas have eyelike patterns that usually give it an angry look. If the “eyes” are droopy and appear sad, it is said to be a sign that a heavy rainfall is on its way.
Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.
Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.
Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.
It intimidates foes with the large eyelike patterns on its antennae. Because it can’t fly if its wings get wet, it shelters itself from rain under large trees and eaves.
The antennae have distinctive patterns that look like eyes. When it rains, they grow heavy, making flight impossible.
It flaps its four wings to hover and fly freely in any direction-- to and fro and sideways.
The antennae have distinctive patterns that look like eyes. When it rains, they grow heavy, making flight impossible.
Its wings and antennae don’t cope well with moisture. After a rain, it faces sunward to dry off.
Masquerain intimidates enemies with the eyelike patterns on its antennas. This Pokémon flaps its four wings to freely fly in any direction—even sideways and backwards—as if it were a helicopter.
Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.
Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.
MASQUERAIN intimidates enemies with the eyelike patterns on its antennas. This POKéMON flaps its four wings to freely fly in any direction - even sideways and backwards - as if it were a helicopter.
MASQUERAIN’s antennas have eyelike patterns that usually give it an angry look. If the “eyes” are droopy and appear sad, it is said to be a sign that a heavy rainfall is on its way.
It flaps its four wings to hover and fly freely in any direction-- to and fro and sideways.
It intimidates enemies with the eye-like patterns on its antennae. Its four wings allow it to fly in any direction.
Its thin, winglike antennae are highly absorbent. It waits out rainy days in tree hollows.
Masquerain intimidates enemies with the eyelike patterns of its eyespots. If that doesn’t work, it deftly makes its escape on its set of four wings.
Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.
Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.
Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.
It flaps its four wings to hover and fly freely in any direction--to and fro and sideways.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Masquerain’s antennas have eyelike patterns that usually give it an angry look. If the “eyes” are droopy and appear sad, it is said to be a sign that a heavy rainfall is on its way.
